course_description.overview: The German edition of L'Eroica features routes through the scenic Rheingau wine region along the Rhine, with distances of approximately 43 km, 80 km, and 130 km on vintage bikes over hilly terrain with 2150m of climbing on the long route[1][3].

key_segments.name: Hohe Wurzel from Wiesbaden
key_segments.description: 6.9% average gradient climb in the region[1]

key_segments.name: Hausen vor der Höhe from Kiedrich

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Challenging hilly routes evoking heroic cycling eras, with multiple climbs through vineyards and castles on vintage bikes[2][3].

logistics.getting_there: Eltville am Rhein is accessible by train from Frankfurt (approx. 45 min) or car via A3 motorway[1].
logistics.accommodation: Plentiful in Rheingau wine region; book early for festival weekend[4].
logistics.registration_details: Online via official Eroica sites; part of global series with limited spots for 1000 riders[1][2].
logistics.bike_logistics: Vintage bikes required (pre-1987); transport own or rent locally; no modern gearing allowed[2].
logistics.local_considerations: Three-day festival atmosphere; expect crowds, wine tastings, and traffic closures[4].

final_verdict.one_liner: Vintage cycling festival through Rhine vineyards with heroic climbs and retro charm.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Enthusiasts of classic bikes seeking adventure, culture, and fatigue in a festive setting.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for the unique Eroica experience blending history, scenery, and community.
pro_tips: Train on vintage bike for steel frame fatigue.
Book accommodation early for festival crowds.
Study climbs like Hohe Wurzel for pacing.
